event: Andrew W. Mellon dies (b. 1855)
Avalon Foundation established by Ailsa Mellon Bruce
Old Dominion Foundation established by Paul Mellon
IRS tax-exempt status conferred (continuity of Avalon)
Avalon and Old Dominion Foundations merge as Andrew W. Mellon Foundation; combined assets ~$273M
Charles S. Hamilton Jr. becomes first president
Nathan Pusey becomes president
John E. Sawyer becomes president
Total assets reach ~$880M
William G. Bowen becomes president
Mellon Mays Undergraduate Fellowship launches
JSTOR established as a Mellon initiative; later spun out as ITHAKA
Mellon Mays Fellowship renamed in honor of Benjamin E. Mays
Don Michael Randel becomes president
Earl Lewis becomes president
$30M endowment challenge grant to National Gallery of Art (75th anniversary)
Elizabeth Alexander appointed president (effective summer 2018)
Kathryn A. Hall succeeds Danielle S. Allen as Board Chair
Strategic shift announcement: foundation reorients all grant-making toward social justice
Rebrand: "The Andrew W. Mellon Foundation" becomes "Mellon Foundation" (Pentagram)
$300M social bond issuance to fund COVID emergency grants
Monuments Project announced with $250M five-year commitment
Creatives Rebuild New York announced; $115M Mellon + $5M Ford + $5M SNF
$5M ALAM (Advancing Latinx Art in Museums) initiative with Ford Getty Terra
Monuments Project commitment doubled to $500M total
Frontera Culture Fund launched (US-Mexico borderlands)
Affirming Multivocal Humanities inaugural cohort ($18M+)
Higher Learning paid internships at five large public universities ($25M)
FY2024: ~650 grants paid totaling ~$540M (annual report) / $635.4M (990-PF Part I)
